findings.title: No test for operations on non-existent table
findings.code_snippet:
await store.createTable('memories', DIMENSION)
const vectors = [
  makeVector('v1', [0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4], 'first memory'),
]
findings.description: VectorStore.insert, search, delete, and count all call this.db.openTable(tableName) which will throw if the table does not exist. No test verifies the error behavior when operating on a table that was never created. This is an important error path -- callers need to know what exception to expect.
findings.suggestion: Add a test case: `it('throws when operating on non-existent table', async () => { await expect(store.search('nonexistent', [0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4], 5)).rejects.toThrow(); })` covering search, insert, delete, and count on a table name that was never created.

findings.title: No test for createTable idempotency
findings.description: VectorStore.createTable contains explicit idempotency logic (lines 26-29 of vector-store.ts: checks existing table names and returns early if present). This behavior is never tested. If the idempotency guard breaks, callers will get errors when creating tables that already exist.
findings.suggestion: Add a test: `it('createTable is idempotent', async () => { await store.createTable('memories', DIMENSION); await store.insert('memories', [makeVector('v1', [0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4], 'data')]); await store.createTable('memories', DIMENSION); const count = await store.count('memories'); expect(count).toBe(1); })` -- verifying the second call does not destroy existing data.

findings.title: No test for dimension mismatch in vector search
findings.description: All tests use DIMENSION=4 consistently. There is no test verifying behavior when a query vector has a different dimension than the stored vectors (e.g., inserting dimension-4 vectors but searching with a dimension-3 query). LanceDB may throw or produce incorrect results. Since vector dimension mismatches are a common runtime error in embedding systems, this boundary should be tested.
findings.suggestion: Add a test: `it('handles dimension mismatch in search query', async () => { await store.createTable('memories', DIMENSION); await store.insert('memories', [makeVector('v1', [1,0,0,0], 'data')]); await expect(store.search('memories', [1,0,0], 2)).rejects.toThrow(); })` -- or verify that LanceDB handles it gracefully and document the behavior.

findings.title: No test for duplicate conversation ID insertion
findings.code_snippet:
const conv: Conversation = {
  id: 'c1',
  role: 'user',
  content: 'Hello Anima',
  createdAt: Date.now(),
}
store.insertConversation(conv)
findings.description: DocumentStore.insertConversation uses a plain INSERT with a PRIMARY KEY on id. Inserting a duplicate id will throw a SQLite constraint error. No test verifies this behavior. Callers need to know whether duplicate insertion is silently handled, throws, or overwrites.
findings.suggestion: Add a test: `it('throws on duplicate conversation id', () => { const conv = { id: 'c1', role: 'user', content: 'first', createdAt: Date.now() }; store.insertConversation(conv); expect(() => store.insertConversation(conv)).toThrow(); })` -- or if the intent is to allow overwrites, change to INSERT OR REPLACE and test idempotency.

findings.title: No test for empty collections (getTodos, getRecentConversations)
findings.description: Neither getTodos() nor getRecentConversations() is tested against an empty database. While the behavior is likely correct (return empty array), explicitly testing empty-state queries documents the contract and prevents regressions if the SQL changes.
findings.suggestion: Add tests: `it('returns empty array when no conversations exist', () => { expect(store.getRecentConversations(10)).toEqual([]); })` and `it('returns empty array when no todos exist', () => { expect(store.getTodos()).toEqual([]); })`.

positive_observations: Both test suites use real databases (LanceDB on disk, better-sqlite3 on disk) with no mocks, fakes, or in-memory substitutes -- fully aligned with Dev/Prod Parity principle
Persistence/reconnect tests verify data survives close and reopen, proving durable storage contracts
DocumentStore intimacy tests cover the floor-at-zero boundary condition (negative delta beyond zero)
Proper test isolation via temp directories with cleanup in afterEach prevents cross-test contamination
VectorStore tests verify search result ordering by distance, not just presence -- testing actual semantic behavior
